doc:
Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_inexflag_p (mpfr/src/exceptions.c L366-L373). Three-line C body: reads the global __gmpfr_flags register and returns __gmpfr_flags & MPFR_FLAGS_INEXACT (bit 8) as an int (0 or non-zero). The TS port returns a boolean per ADR 0001 idiomatic-TS lift for the _p predicate family.

Wire form takes `mask: bigint` describing the desired pre-test flag state. The driver clears + sets mask before reading the predicate; the TS port mirrors that via setFlags + getFlags & MPFR_FLAGS_INEXACT.

Class 'misc' (1000ms per-case budget). Tag minimums per Rule 7: happy 20, edge 30, adv 12, fuzz 50, mined 5.

Note: bit value is 8 (0x08). Common bug: testing against MPFR_FLAGS_NAN (4) or MPFR_FLAGS_ERANGE (16).
divergence_from_c:
Three divergences:

  1. Return type: C int (0 or non-zero) -> TS boolean (ADR 0001 idiomatic-TS lift).

  2. Parameter list: C void -> TS (mask: bigint). The wire form encodes the prior global flag state. Same composition as the rest of the *_p predicate family (erangeflag_p, nanflag_p, divby0_p, ...).

  3. Global flag state: C reads __gmpfr_flags directly; TS routes through getFlags() after setFlags(mask).
refs: mpfr/src/exceptions.c L366-L373 -- the C reference body.
src/internal/mpfr/flags.ts -- TS flag register; getFlags() & 8n is the canonical predicate.
